evidence_for: docs/regimes/us_senate.md (goal 060 spec leg) — Senate eFD PTR session dance, search endpoint contract, report-page anatomy, fixture pins, client-fingerprint probe matrix

identification.user_agent_attempted: govfolio.io research (contact: ssm.leo@outlook.com) — REJECTED (Akamai 403, see probe_matrix)
identification.user_agent_used: stock Chromium 149 UA (Playwright-bundled browser, UA untouched)
identification.contact_identification: From: ssm.leo@outlook.com request header on every request of every successful session
identification.rationale: the host's bot manager mechanically rejects any non-stock UA string and any non-browser TLS fingerprint; identification therefore rides the standard From header (RFC 9110 §10.1.2) instead of the UA. Recorded as a politeness deviation, not a stealth choice: contact identification was present on every request that reached the origin.

politeness.notes: each 403 was followed by a configuration change, never a blind retry; no 429 or throttling observed at concurrency 1 with >=2s spacing; static assets loaded by the real browser during page navigations are not separately throttled (normal browser behavior)

probe_matrix_client_fingerprint.ua: Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; govfolio.io research; ...) + browser Accept headers
stock Chrome 137 UA string + Sec-Fetch headers
probe_matrix_client_fingerprint.result: 403 — UA string alone does not pass; TLS/HTTP fingerprint is checked
403 — custom UA suffix on a real browser is ALSO rejected (UA/client-hints consistency check)

probe_matrix_client_fingerprint.client: Playwright APIRequestContext (Node HTTP stack, non-browser TLS)
probe_matrix_client_fingerprint.result: 403 even with a valid, agreement-accepted session cookie set (hybrid probe) — GET-HTML paths are fingerprint-gated regardless of cookies
302 / 200 — POST endpoints passed from the non-browser stack in every attempt
200 (no session required on the media host)

session_dance.result: 302 Location: /search/home/ when the session has not accepted the agreement; the redirect target sets csrftoken (~1y expiry, not HttpOnly) and one opaque session cookie whose NAME and VALUE are both 32-char hex strings (e.g. 33a5c6d97f299a223cb6fc3925909ef7=234ecb30977c50e635749ba9af5ace7d; HttpOnly, Secure, session-scoped)

200 agreement page: <form id="agreement_form" action="" method="POST"> with checkbox name=prohibition_agreement value=1 (id agree_statement) and hidden csrfmiddlewaretoken (64-char masked Django token, distinct from the 32-char csrftoken cookie); page JS submits the form on checkbox click
302 Location: /search/ — agreement acceptance is stored server-side against the opaque session cookie; no new cookie is set

200 search form page (post-agreement). Field legend: filer_type checkbox values 1=Senator, 4=Candidate, 5=Former Senator; report_type checkbox values 7=Annual, 11=Periodic Transactions, 10=Due Date Extension, 14=Blind Trusts, 15=Other Documents; date fields submitted_start_date/submitted_end_date; the page's own DataTables config POSTs to /search/report/data/ with exactly the documented body fields

session_dance.form: draw, start, length, report_types='[11]', filer_types='[1]', submitted_start_date='MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM:SS', submitted_end_date, candidate_state, senator_state, office_id, first_name, last_name, order[0][column], order[0][dir], columns[N][data]
session_dance.required: X-CSRFToken header = csrftoken COOKIE value (not the form token), Referer: /search/, X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest
session_dance.result: 200 JSON {draw, recordsTotal, recordsFiltered, data: [[first_name, last_name, office, 'title', date_submitted MM/DD/YYYY], ...]}

200 electronic PTR HTML (session required; fingerprint-gated). /search/view/paper/{uuid}/ serves a GIF-image carousel page instead; images live on https://efd-media-public.senate.gov/media/... (no session)

variance_tests.cross_session_refetch: ptr/4b69867f fetched in a separate fresh session (new agreement dance): byte-identical; ptr/4aa0094d, ptr/727b4eb6, ptr/a9754ff5 each re-fetched in a second session: byte-identical
variance_tests.token_scan: 0 occurrences of 'csrf' in any fetched /search/view/ptr/ page — view pages embed no session-variant markup
variance_tests.conclusion: raw-byte sha256 pinning is sound for /search/view/ptr/ pages today; normalization fallback rule documented in us_senate.md section 7 in case the host ever starts injecting per-session markup

response_headers_view_page.note: captured on pin-check GETs (dance6): no ETag and no Last-Modified on /search/view/ptr/ responses; conditional GETs are NOT available on report pages — cache by sha after a single fetch (pages proved byte-stable) and never re-fetch a stored report
response_headers_view_page.robots_txt: HTTP 404 under the working browser configuration (styled 404 page). The earlier curl 403s were the bot manager, not a robots policy. No robots.txt exists.

historical_depth_probe.query: report_types=[11], filer_types=[], submitted_start_date=01/01/2012, order by date_submitted asc, length 10
historical_depth_probe.earliest: Periodic Transaction Report for 07/25/2012, BENJAMIN L CARDIN (paper, /search/view/paper/CDFDAF62-18EA-4298-B0C5-62085A6EC3CD/)
